faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Peekskill home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, webbing in closet corners, tiny cream-colored larvae on fabrics, and small brown moths flying around wardrobes or storage areas. You may also notice casings or cocoons on clothing or shelving in dark, undisturbed areas of your Peekskill home.

faqs.question: What is the typical cost of clothes moth control in Peekskill?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Peekskill typically range from $550 to $5,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of treatment areas required. Larger homes with extensive closet spaces or historic properties with multiple storage areas may require more comprehensive treatment plans at the higher end of the pricing range.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Most clothes moth treatments show immediate results for adult moths, but complete elimination of larvae and eggs may take 2-4 weeks. Our multi-phase approach includes initial treatment, follow-up inspections, and preventative measures to ensure all life stages are eliminated. We recommend monitoring for 60-90 days to confirm complete eradication from your Peekskill property.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths damage items other than clothing?
faqs.answer: Yes, clothes moths can damage any items containing natural fibers including upholstered furniture, carpets, rugs, curtains, blankets, tapestries, and even taxidermy. They're particularly attracted to soiled fabrics with perspiration, food stains, or body oils. In Peekskill's older homes with vintage furnishings and wool carpeting, protecting these items is especially important to preserve their value and condition.
